_curation_rules.uncertain_event_rule: If an event's 2026 occurrence is in question (postponed, cancelled, indefinitely deferred), set is_active=false. Reactivate when official dates are confirmed by a verifiable source. is_active=true is required for Content Plan to surface this event.
_curation_rules.secondary_confidence_meaning: source_confidence='secondary' means the event runs annually but a definitive 2026 date page wasn't independently sourced at curation time. All 'secondary' entries in this file are seasonal_range (month-windowed) by design — the month-range modeling absorbs date imprecision, so a secondary-confidence seasonal entry can still surface accurately to Content Plan without risking a misleading specific date.
_curation_rules.translation_confidence_meaning: _translation_notes flag each name_ar as 'verified: <source>' or 'best_effort: <reasoning>'. Best-effort translations are descriptive Arabic phrases (typically for cross-country signals like summer_heat_peak or back_to_school that have no canonical event name).

_meta.batch_description: Batch 1 (22, approved with 5 fixes applied): 7 shared lunar + 8 Saudi + 7 UAE. Batch 2 (29, awaiting review): 6 Qatar + 3 Kuwait + 5 Oman + 7 Bahrain + 8 cross-country shared. Total 51 entries.
_meta.generated_by: claude-opus-4-7 + manual cross-reference against Wikipedia AR, visitsaudi.com/ar/, visitdubai.com/ar/, visitsharjah.com, visitqatar.com, foundingday.sa, experiencealula.com, diriyahseason.sa, dohabookfair.qa, culture.gov.bh, gov.om, katara.net, Amiri Diwan Qatar, DCT Abu Dhabi
_meta.architectural_principle_batch_2: Per user direction: 'Does this event have commercial behavior patterns that cross GCC borders, or is it purely tied to one country's identity/geography?' National days + country-specific heritage festivals = country-pinned. F1 races + Khareef + cross-GCC retail/climate moments = country_iso=NULL with personalization via industries_high_relevance / industries_low_relevance. Bahrain GP, Saudi GP, Abu Dhabi GP all live as shared per this principle (vs. Bahrain National Day, Saudi National Day, UAE National Day which stay country-pinned).
_meta.next_batch: Phase 1D — seeding script + tests. Pending user approval of Batch 2.
_meta.reference_file_note: The uploaded /mnt/user-data/uploads/gcc_cultural_calendar_comprehensive.json was not accessible to the agent during this batch (Linux-style path on Windows host). Entries were built from the explicit checklist in the Phase 1C prompt + independent web verification. The user may wish to reconcile this file against the upload before approving.

_entry_notes.mawlid_al_nabi: VERIFICATION FLAG: Mawlid is NOT a public holiday in Saudi Arabia (Wahhabi/Salafi tradition rejects celebration of the Prophet's birthday) and is generally low-key/avoided in mainstream Saudi public life. It IS an official public holiday in the UAE and most other GCC states. Modeled here as country_iso=NULL (shared lunar) because the date itself is shared, but content_guidelines.avoid explicitly flags Saudi audience sensitivity. Consider whether to split this into per-country entries in a future iteration, or to drop country_iso=NULL and create AE/QA/KW/OM/BH variants in Batch 2.
_entry_notes.frieze_abu_dhabi: DATE CORRECTION: Phase 1C prompt listed this as 'Apr, arts/luxury'. The actual event runs in November (2026 inaugural Frieze edition: 19–22 Nov). Event_key renamed from `abu_dhabi_art` to `frieze_abu_dhabi` per user decision in Batch 1 review (the Frieze rebrand is the forward-looking brand identity).

uae_commemoration_day.decision: Pinned to Dec 1 with duration_days=1 per user's Batch 1 review (the public-holiday observance is the surfacing target).
uae_commemoration_day.commemorates_event_date: November 30 (date of first Emirati soldier's death, 1971). The actual commemoration ceremony — flag at half-mast 8am→11:30am, 1-minute national silence at 11:30 — still occurs on Nov 30; Dec 1 is the day-off observance. Content Plan suggestions tied to this entry should account for the fact that tributes/condolence content from large brands typically lands on Nov 30, while public/family content surges on Dec 1.

_entry_notes.riyadh_season: Riyadh Season 2025-26 actually opens Oct 10 and runs through ~May 2 (Boulevard World closes May 2). Modeled here as seasonal Oct-Mar per the Phase 1C checklist; this captures the bulk of activity. If you'd like the wider Oct-Apr window for late-season tail, say so.
_entry_notes.alula_season: Officially branded 'AlUla Moments' for the 2025-26 season. The full season runs ~Sep through the following summer per Experience AlUla. Modeled here as seasonal Sep-Apr (8-month window) — wider than the Phase 1C prompt's 'Oct-Apr' to match the official Sep start, but trimmed before summer to keep the active-tourism window honest.
_entry_notes.winter_at_tantora: 2025-26 edition ran Dec 18 2025 → Jan 10 2026. Modeled as seasonal Dec-Jan to match this annual pattern.
_entry_notes.janadriyah_festival: Originally an annual heritage festival run by the Saudi National Guard since 1985; transferred to Ministry of Culture in 2019. 2026 edition (31st) confirmed to have run Feb 1-17 in Riyadh — verified via DCT Abu Dhabi press release announcing the UAE pavilion at the festival. Source confidence upgraded from 'secondary' to 'verified' during Batch 1 review.

hajj_season.decision: Changed from country_iso=SA to country_iso=NULL per user's Batch 1 architectural correction: 'GCC businesses outside SA serve Hajj travelers with specialized products. Relevance is personalization (industries_*), not geography.'
hajj_season.industries_split: industries_high_relevance enumerates the actual Hajj-serving verticals (travel_agency, tour_operator, airline, hotel, luggage_retail, pharmacy, telecom, money_exchange, religious_tourism, hospitality). industries_low_relevance explicitly excludes fashion_beauty, entertainment, nightlife, alcohol_beverage to prevent culturally tone-deaf surfacing. Eid al-Adha (which immediately follows) remains shared lunar with country_iso=NULL.

_entry_notes.saudi_founding_day: Established as a public holiday in January 2022 by royal decree of King Salman, commemorating Imam Muhammad bin Saud's ascendancy in 1727. Distinct from National Day (which commemorates the 1932 unification under King Abdulaziz). Both are now annual non-religious public holidays.
_entry_notes.qatar_national_sports_day: Modeled as seasonal_range Feb-Feb (not fixed_gregorian) because the date shifts year to year — it's officially the 2nd Tuesday of February. Hardcoding a specific Feb day would be wrong by 2027. year_specific_notes pins the actual 2026 date (Feb 10) for that year's surfacing.
_entry_notes.oman_sultan_accession_day: Canonical date is Jan 11 (Sultan Haitham took the throne Jan 11 2020). However Oman's advance-announcement public-holiday policy can shift the OBSERVED day-off year to year. In 2026, the observed holiday is Thursday Jan 15 (likely shifted from Sunday Jan 11 to create a Thu-Fri-Sat extended weekend). Stored Jan 11 as the canonical date; year_specific_notes carries the observed-day shift. Content Plan should surface Jan 11 for thematic content but flag the Jan 15 holiday for behaviorally-tied content.
_entry_notes.spring_of_culture: 2026 edition postponed; reactivate when official dates confirmed. is_active=true required for Content Plan to surface this event. The 20th edition was scheduled for March-May 2026 but has been POSTPONED (no rescheduled date announced as of curation time). Kept entry shape with seasonal Mar-May (the historical pattern) and source_confidence=secondary so the row's available the moment new dates land — just flip is_active back to true. Worth a follow-up search closer to the originally-planned start.
_entry_notes.pearling_festival: VERIFICATION FLAG: A standalone annual 'Pearling Festival' in Bahrain wasn't independently confirmable during curation — pearling heritage in Bahrain is sometimes programmed as part of the Bahrain Summer Festival (Jul-Aug) and sometimes appears as standalone events in Muharraq district. Kept the entry per the original Phase 1C checklist with source_confidence=secondary; user should verify with Bahrain Authority for Culture whether to keep as standalone November programming or fold into Summer Festival.
_entry_notes.khareef_salalah: Moved from Oman (country_iso=OM) to cross-country shared (country_iso=NULL) per user's Batch 2 architectural principle. Rationale: Khareef tourism is driven heavily by GCC travelers (especially UAE residents escaping summer heat — Oman Air launched dedicated DXB-Salalah flights for the season). The behavioral pattern is cross-border. industries_high_relevance enumerates tourism + travel verticals; relevance is personalization, not geography.
_entry_notes.f1_abu_dhabi_grand_prix: Modeled as country_iso=NULL (cross-country shared) per user's Batch 2 architectural principle — F1 races attract cross-GCC luxury/sports audiences. industries_high_relevance includes sports, hospitality, luxury_retail, and premium hotels per the user's example. Located in UAE but relevance is cross-border, not geographic.
_entry_notes.f1_saudi_arabia_grand_prix: Same principle as Abu Dhabi GP — country_iso=NULL with industries-based personalization. Located in Saudi Arabia (Jeddah Corniche Circuit) but audience and commercial behavior is cross-GCC luxury/sports.
_entry_notes.black_friday_white_friday: Note the Arabic 'White Friday' (الجمعة البيضاء) rebrand is the regional-canonical form, pioneered by Souq.com in 2014. Modeled as cross-country shared because the retail behavior pattern is uniform across GCC. Caveat: Nov 27 2026 (likely Black/White Friday date) is followed by Nov 30 (UAE Commemoration Day) — UAE-targeted campaigns should pause promotional content on Nov 30.
_entry_notes.new_year_eve: Cross-country shared but with NUANCED industries_low_relevance: alcohol-themed celebration content fits UAE/Bahrain but not Saudi/Kuwait/Qatar (all dry markets). content_guidelines flags this directly so the personalization layer can decide based on the audience's primary market.
_entry_notes.summer_heat_peak: Climate signal, not a named event. Modeled as cross-country shared because the behavioral content shift (audience moves indoors, midday outdoor content collapses) is uniform across GCC. source_url is null because this is an inferred climate pattern, not an external event.
_entry_notes.back_to_school: Commercial moment, not a named event. Modeled as cross-country shared because school start dates cluster late-Aug/early-Sep across GCC (varying by curriculum but the window holds).
_entry_notes.winter_outdoor_season: Climate signal, not a named event. Modeled as cross-country shared because the comfortable-weather window (Dec-Feb) drives outdoor lifestyle content uniformly across GCC.

_translation_notes.ramadan: verified: Arabic Wikipedia uses 'شهر رمضان' or simply 'رمضان' — used 'شهر رمضان' for clarity in content contexts
_translation_notes.laylat_al_qadr: verified: 'ليلة القدر' is the canonical MSA name (Quranic origin, Surat Al-Qadr)
_translation_notes.eid_al_fitr: verified: 'عيد الفطر' — universal MSA, no regional variation
_translation_notes.islamic_new_year: verified: 'رأس السنة الهجرية' — standard MSA. 'العام الهجري الجديد' also seen but the former is more common in calendar contexts.
_translation_notes.mawlid_al_nabi: verified: 'المولد النبوي الشريف' — full formal name. 'المولد النبوي' (without 'الشريف') is also widely used; I picked the fuller form because it's the more reverent/formal version.
_translation_notes.saudi_national_day: verified: 'اليوم الوطني السعودي' — official, matches Saudi gov usage
_translation_notes.saudi_founding_day: verified: 'يوم التأسيس' — official, matches foundingday.sa wordmark
_translation_notes.riyadh_season: verified: 'موسم الرياض' — official, matches Riyadh Season Instagram + visitsaudi.com/ar/
_translation_notes.janadriyah_festival: verified: 'مهرجان الجنادرية' — common shortened form; full official name is 'مهرجان الجنادرية للتراث والثقافة' (Janadriyah Festival for Heritage and Culture). Used the shortened form for UI compactness; full name in content_guidelines.tone if needed.
_translation_notes.diriyah_season: verified: 'موسم الدرعية' — official, matches diriyahseason.sa + visitsaudi.com/ar/
_translation_notes.winter_at_tantora: verified: 'شتاء طنطورة' — official, matches Experience AlUla Arabic site. 'طنطورة' refers to the traditional AlUla sundial, kept as Arabic transliteration.
_translation_notes.alula_season: best_effort: 'موسم العلا' is the direct translation matching the seasonal naming convention (Riyadh Season → موسم الرياض, etc.). The brand also uses 'لحظات العلا' (AlUla Moments) for the 2025-26 program. Picked the seasonal-pattern name for consistency; flagging for user review.
_translation_notes.uae_national_day: verified: 'اليوم الوطني لدولة الإمارات' — official, matches UAE gov usage. Shorter 'اليوم الوطني الإماراتي' also acceptable.
_translation_notes.uae_commemoration_day: verified: 'يوم الشهيد' (Yawm Al-Shahid, lit. 'Martyr's Day') is the official Arabic name. English 'Commemoration Day' is the diplomatic-export rendering; Arabic-language UAE government communications consistently use 'يوم الشهيد'. Using the official Arabic form.
_translation_notes.uae_flag_day: verified: 'يوم العلم' — official, matches mediaoffice.abudhabi/ar usage
_translation_notes.dubai_shopping_festival: verified: 'مهرجان دبي للتسوق' — official, matches visitdubai.com/ar/
_translation_notes.dubai_summer_surprises: verified: 'مفاجآت صيف دبي' — official, matches visitdubai.com/ar/
_translation_notes.sharjah_heritage_days: verified: 'أيام الشارقة التراثية' — official, matches visitsharjah.com Arabic pages
_translation_notes.frieze_abu_dhabi: best_effort: 'فريز أبوظبي' is the direct transliteration following Frieze's brand-localization pattern in other regions. The Arabic naming for the 2026 inaugural Frieze edition is not yet established in official Arabic communications. Following the user's Batch 1 decision to rename to `frieze_abu_dhabi`, used the Frieze-aligned Arabic name as well. Flagging for user review — may need a different rendering when Frieze's official Arabic launch materials drop closer to Nov 2026.
_translation_notes.qatar_national_day: verified: 'اليوم الوطني لدولة قطر' — official, matches visitqatar.com Arabic + Amiri Diwan
_translation_notes.qatar_national_sports_day: verified: 'اليوم الرياضي للدولة' — official short form. Full official: 'اليوم الرياضي لدولة قطر'. Used the shorter, more common public-facing form.
_translation_notes.katara_dhow_festival: verified: 'مهرجان كتارا للمحامل التقليدية' — official, matches katara.net Arabic. 'محامل' (mahamil) is the Arabic plural for traditional wooden boats (dhows).
_translation_notes.souq_waqif_spring_festival: best_effort: 'مهرجان سوق واقف الربيعي' is the standard descriptive Arabic name. Note that several Souq Waqif events run annually (Spring Festival, International Dates Exhibition, etc.) — this entry covers the carnival-style Spring Festival specifically. Confirm with visitqatar.com closer to event time.
_translation_notes.doha_international_book_fair: verified: 'معرض الدوحة الدولي للكتاب' — official, matches dohabookfair.qa
_translation_notes.ajyal_film_festival: verified: 'مهرجان أجيال السينمائي' — official, matches Doha Film Institute Arabic
_translation_notes.kuwait_national_day: verified: 'اليوم الوطني الكويتي' — official, matches Kuwait gov sources
_translation_notes.kuwait_liberation_day: verified: 'يوم التحرير الكويتي' — official, matches Kuwait gov sources
_translation_notes.hala_february: verified: 'مهرجان هلا فبراير' — official, this is the standard local Arabic form. Sometimes spelled 'هلا فبراير' without 'مهرجان' (festival) prefix.
_translation_notes.oman_national_day: verified: 'العيد الوطني العماني' — official. Note: Oman uses 'العيد' (eid, festival/feast) rather than 'اليوم' (day) — a distinctive local usage worth preserving.
_translation_notes.oman_sultan_accession_day: verified: 'يوم تولي السلطان هيثم بن طارق مقاليد الحكم' is the formal full Arabic name (literally 'Day His Majesty Sultan Haitham bin Tarik took over the reins of governance'). Shorter forms in casual usage: 'يوم جلوس السلطان' or 'يوم التولي'. Used the formal form to match Royal Decree 88/2022 phrasing.
_translation_notes.salalah_tourism_festival: verified: 'مهرجان صلالة السياحي' — official. The festival also gets called 'مهرجان صلالة الخريفي' (Khareef Festival) in some communications, but the tourism-festival branding is the official Ministry of Heritage & Tourism name.
_translation_notes.jabal_akhdar_rose_festival: verified: 'مهرجان ورد الجبل الأخضر' — official, matches Dakhliyah Governorate Arabic announcement
_translation_notes.bahrain_accession_day: verified: 'يوم جلوس جلالة الملك حمد' — official, matches Bahrain gov sources
_translation_notes.spring_of_culture: verified: 'ربيع الثقافة' — official, matches culture.gov.bh Arabic
_translation_notes.bahrain_heritage_festival: verified: 'مهرجان البحرين للتراث' — official, matches Bahrain Authority for Culture
_translation_notes.bahrain_summer_festival: verified: 'مهرجان صيف البحرين' — official, matches culture.gov.bh Arabic
_translation_notes.pearling_festival: verified: 'مهرجان اللؤلؤ' — descriptive form. The UNESCO designation uses 'طريق اللؤلؤ' (Pearling Path) but the festival programming itself uses the festival framing.
_translation_notes.bahrain_food_festival: verified: 'مهرجان البحرين للأطعمة' — official, matches Bahrain Tourism. English brand name 'Flavors Around the World' doesn't get an Arabic translation (it's a brand mark).
_translation_notes.summer_heat_peak: best_effort: 'ذروة الحر الصيفي في الخليج' is a descriptive Arabic phrase, not an official branded term — this entry is a climate-driven Content Plan signal, not a named event, so there is no canonical Arabic name to verify against. Constructed descriptive phrase using standard MSA.
_translation_notes.back_to_school: best_effort: 'موسم العودة إلى المدارس' is the standard Arabic phrasing used in regional retail marketing. Not a 'verified' source-canonical name because this is a commercial moment, not a named event.
_translation_notes.winter_outdoor_season: best_effort: 'موسم الأنشطة الخارجية الشتوية' is a descriptive Arabic phrase. Not a branded event — climate-driven Content Plan signal.
_translation_notes.black_friday_white_friday: verified: 'الجمعة البيضاء' (White Friday) is the established Arabic regional branding, pioneered by Souq.com in 2014 and now used by Amazon.sa, Noon, and most regional retailers. The 'Black Friday' form (الجمعة السوداء) carries unfortunate negative connotations in Arabic (death, mourning), which is why the White Friday rebrand stuck. Used the regional-canonical Arabic form.
_translation_notes.new_year_eve: verified: 'ليلة رأس السنة الميلادية' — standard MSA. The 'الميلادية' (Gregorian/Christian-calendar) suffix distinguishes from Islamic New Year (رأس السنة الهجرية) — important disambiguation.
_translation_notes.khareef_salalah: verified: 'خريف صلالة' — official, the universal regional usage. 'خريف' literally means 'autumn' but in Omani usage refers specifically to the monsoon season.
_translation_notes.f1_abu_dhabi_grand_prix: verified: 'جائزة أبوظبي الكبرى للفورمولا 1' — official, matches Arabic F1 broadcasts and Yas Marina Circuit Arabic communications. 'الجائزة الكبرى' = Grand Prix.
_translation_notes.f1_saudi_arabia_grand_prix: verified: 'جائزة السعودية الكبرى للفورمولا 1' — official, matches Arabic F1 broadcasts and Saudi Motorsport Company communications

avoid: public daytime eating or drinking
loud party scenes or nightclub aesthetics
irreverent humor around fasting
scantily-clad models
alcohol references however incidental
music-heavy posts during daytime hours

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: strongly evening-heavy; daytime engagement collapses; spike around iftar (~sunset) and again post-taraweeh prayer
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: night-centered; supermarket trolleys peak immediately after iftar; fashion/abaya/decor purchases anticipatory in the 2 weeks prior
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: significantly elevated in evenings; Snapchat/TikTok consumption highest in GCC of any month
audience_behavior.food_delivery_spike: extreme — both at iftar (~sunset) and suhoor (pre-dawn)

year_specific_notes.2026: Ramadan 1447 AH falls in winter (~Feb 18 → Mar 19 2026 per UMQ). Cooler evenings enable outdoor majlis/iftar content; audience more physically active post-iftar than in prior summer cycles.

avoid: promotional/sales-led messaging
celebratory or party imagery
any framing that overshadows the spiritual significance
humor
prefer: zakat al-fitr reminders for charity sector

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: late-night spike; many Muslims observe i'tikaf (mosque retreat) so social use shifts to brief reflective shares rather than scrolling
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: muted; spending pivots to charitable giving (zakat al-fitr)
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; reflective content outperforms promotional

year_specific_notes.2026: Falls on the night of approximately 16 → 17 March 2026 (27 Ramadan 1447 AH per UMQ). Traditionally observed on one of the odd nights of the last 10 days of Ramadan; the 27th is the most widely-observed canonical date in GCC.

avoid: discount-only messaging without family/spiritual context
generic 'happy holidays' translations
imagery that conflates Eid with Christmas/New Year aesthetics
alcohol references
irreverent humor

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: morning spike unusual for GCC — driven by Eid prayer and family visit posts; secondary evening peak
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: anticipatory peak in the 7-14 days prior (Eid clothing, gifts); muted during the holiday itself except food/sweets
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: highest engagement day of the post-Ramadan month; selfies, family videos dominate

year_specific_notes.2026: 1 Shawwal 1447 AH ≈ 20 March 2026 per UMQ. Saudi Supreme Court announcement may shift by ±1 day based on moon sighting. Most GCC states observe a 3-day public holiday window.

avoid: promotional/sales-led messaging
celebratory or party imagery
frivolous humor
any framing that overshadows the day's spiritual weight (it is the pinnacle of Hajj)
prefer: pilgrim imagery from Mount Arafat (where rights-cleared)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: daytime-leaning (many Muslims fast and break fast quietly); spiritual posts outperform commercial
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: muted
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; quote/Quran verse content outperforms

avoid: graphic depictions of qurban (sacrifice) — sensitive even when culturally relevant
discount-only messaging without family/spiritual context
imagery that crosses into vegan-shaming or celebration-of-meat in a way that excludes other dietary preferences
alcohol references
prefer: themes of charity (one-third of qurban is for those in need)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: morning prayer + family-visit posts dominate; secondary evening peak; outbound travel posts strong
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: anticipatory peak 7-14 days prior (clothing, gifts, livestock); spending pivots to travel/leisure during the holiday
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: high; significant 'away' content (travel, family reunions)
audience_behavior.food_delivery_spike: elevated for catering and sweets; quieter for individual delivery (people are eating at family gatherings)

year_specific_notes.2026: 10 Dhul Hijjah 1447 AH ≈ 27 May 2026 per UMQ. Falls right after Hajj. 4-day public holiday standard across GCC.

avoid: fireworks/champagne aesthetics (those belong to Gregorian New Year, not Hijri)
loud party imagery
year-end retrospectives that lift Western format wholesale
secular celebratory messaging
prefer: reflective content about the year ahead

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: moderate uplift; less commercial activity than the Eids
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: neutral; no anticipatory spike
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; reflective and historical content outperforms

year_specific_notes.2026: 1 Muharram 1448 AH ≈ 15 June 2026 per UMQ (the Hijri new year that begins during Gregorian 2026).

avoid: FOR SAUDI/QATAR AUDIENCES: celebratory or festival framing — Mawlid is NOT observed publicly in Saudi Arabia (Wahhabi/Salafi tradition rejects it) and is similarly low-key in Qatar. Avoid Mawlid hashtags or congratulatory messaging when targeting those markets.
any imagery of the Prophet (universally avoided across all Muslim audiences)
music-heavy or party imagery
prefer: FOR UAE/KUWAIT/OMAN/BAHRAIN AUDIENCES: themes of the Prophet's life and teachings, mosque imagery, calligraphy of the shahada, community lecture/madrasa scenes
for mixed-GCC audiences: neutral reflective content that doesn't take a position on observance

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: highly country-dependent; strong uplift in UAE/Kuwait/Oman/Bahrain, near-baseline in Saudi Arabia and Qatar
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: neutral
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: elevated in observing countries, baseline elsewhere

year_specific_notes.2026: 12 Rabi al-Awwal 1448 AH ≈ 26 August 2026 per UMQ. SA does NOT have a public holiday; UAE does.

avoid: foreign visual elements (no generic 'patriotic' iconography that could read as any country)
mixing with non-Saudi national symbols (e.g. UAE/Qatar flags in the same composition)
generic patriotic clichés or stock-photo 'celebration' imagery
irreverent humor about the kingdom or royal family
any imagery that could be read as critical of Vision 2030 themes
prefer: Saudi flag (green with shahada) — handled with respect, never on the ground or below the waist
modern Vision 2030 themes (NEOM, Diriyah, Riyadh skyline)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evening-heavy; major fireworks shows in Riyadh/Jeddah drive a sharp peak; #اليوم_الوطني_السعودي trends nationally
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for fashion (greenwear), automotive (national-day promotions are a major category), home decor
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: year's-highest day in Saudi Arabia for Instagram and TikTok

year_specific_notes.2026: Marks 96th anniversary of the 1932 unification. Government and large brands typically reveal campaigns 14-21 days in advance.

avoid: conflating with National Day (Sep 23) — Founding Day marks 1727 by Imam Muhammad bin Saud, NOT the 1932 unification
modern Vision 2030 skyscraper imagery (use heritage instead — that's the day's spirit)
foreign aesthetics or generic patriotic stock
Saudi flag with shahada (use historical Saudi state flags or Najdi banner motifs instead — Founding Day branding deliberately uses pre-modern symbols)
prefer: ardha sword dance, falconry, equestrian heritage
the official Founding Day visual identity (palm-frond + sword + horse motif)
earthy tones referencing Najd desert palette

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: heritage-themed content outperforms; expect strong Diriyah-related posts; #يوم_التأسيس trends
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for traditional dress, heritage-themed home decor, gold/jewelry
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: high in Saudi Arabia; family heritage content surges
audience_behavior.food_delivery_spike: elevated for traditional Najdi cuisine (kabsa, jareesh)

year_specific_notes.2026: 5th annual observance since establishment in 2022. Still 'building' as a brand vs. the long-established National Day.

avoid: dated 'Saudi as conservative' framing — Riyadh Season is the headline expression of post-Vision-2030 entertainment opening
any sense that international acts are 'compromising' for Saudi audiences
imagery of the season as foreign-imposed (it is GEA-led, Saudi-branded)
alcohol references (Riyadh Season is alcohol-free)
prefer: Boulevard City and Boulevard World skyline shots
food zone variety (the season hosts hundreds of restaurants)
themed zones (Anime Village, WWE, etc. when topical)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evenings; weekend (Thu-Sat) spike; ride-hailing demand surges around Boulevard zones
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for season-tied merchandise, food/beverage, event tickets
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: sustained 5-month elevation in Saudi Arabia, especially TikTok/Snapchat among 18-34

year_specific_notes.2026: 2025-26 edition opened Oct 10 2025; Boulevard World extension runs to ~May 2 2026. 11 zones, 200+ concerts, 34 exhibitions, 15 world championships.

avoid: modern/futurist aesthetics (this is a heritage festival, not Vision-2030 imagery)
framing as a 'tourist trap' or staged spectacle
any content that flattens regional diversity into a generic 'Saudi' palette — the festival's whole point is showcasing each region's distinct heritage
prefer: region-by-region heritage themes (Najd, Hijaz, Asir, Eastern Province all represented)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evening-heavy; weekend spike
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for traditional crafts, jewelry, regional food products
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate uplift, strongest among 35+ heritage-interested audience

year_specific_notes.2026: 31st edition ran Feb 1-17 2026 in Riyadh (UAE participated). Source confirmed via DCT Abu Dhabi press release on the UAE pavilion.

avoid: lumping it together with Riyadh Season — Diriyah Season is positioned as the heritage/culture counterpart to Riyadh Season's entertainment positioning
modern skyscraper backdrops (use At-Turaif and Najdi mud-brick architecture instead)
loud party aesthetics — this season's vibe is curated, slower, more high-end
prefer: At-Turaif UNESCO heritage site (golden-hour shots are iconic)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evening-heavy; high-engagement content tends to be At-Turaif sunset shots
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for luxury/fine-dining bookings, traditional crafts
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: strong uplift in lifestyle/travel segments; outsized international audience interest

year_specific_notes.2026: 2025-26 edition runs Nov 1 2025 → Mar 23 2026. Theme: 'Where You Experience Legacy'. Drew 1.3M+ visitors mid-season.

avoid: beach/summer aesthetics
any framing that treats AlUla as a 'desert resort' rather than a heritage destination
modern skyscraper imagery
neon/club aesthetics
prefer: Maraya mirrored concert hall (most-photographed UAE-region structure in 2020s)
Hegra (Madain Saleh) rock-cut tombs at golden hour

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: sunset and concert photography drives strong evening peaks; international/expat audience tilts the timezone profile
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: high-value travel bookings (hotels, flights to AlUla airport); concert ticket purchases
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: outsized international engagement vs. typical KSA event

year_specific_notes.2026: 2025-26 edition ran Dec 18 2025 → Jan 10 2026. Concerts at Maraya, AlManshiyah Carnival, Old Town Nights, candlelit classical concerts at AlUla Fort.

avoid: treating AlUla as 'just a tourist site' — the brand positioning is curated cultural destination, not theme park
summer-month content (the season deliberately ends April when temperatures rise)
imagery that hides the landscape behind crowds — solo / small-group framings dominate the brand voice
prefer: Banyan Tree / Habitas / Our Habitas tented stays

audience_behavior.peak_hours: Sunrise + sunset windows dominate engagement curve
audience_behavior.engagement_shift: landscape-photography-driven; international travel audience visible in engagement timing
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: high-value bookings (premium hotels, charter flights)
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate but very high-engagement-per-post (low-volume, high-aspirational segment)

year_specific_notes.2026: 200+ events across the 2025-26 season. Marquee festivals include Winter at Tantora (separate entry), Ancient Kingdoms Festival (Nov), AlUla Tour cycling (Jan 27-31), Desert X (Jan 28-Feb 1).

avoid: any promotional/sales messaging during the pilgrimage window — Saudi authorities are explicit about treating Hajj with reverence
imagery that frames Hajj as 'spectacle' or tourist activity
stock pilgrim imagery without permissioning (Saudi authorities issue official Hajj photography)
showing the Kaaba in any context that doesn't make Hajj/Umrah the subject
any framing that excludes non-pilgrims (most of the local audience are not pilgrims this year)
prefer: respectful coverage of pilgrim journey themes
messages of solidarity with the millions of pilgrims
for Hajj-serving verticals (travel agencies, telcos, pharmacies, money exchanges): practical pre-departure preparation tips, in-Mecca service availability, family-reunion-on-return themes
for non-religious brands: pause cadence on commercial campaigns during the peak days

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: Saudi domestic audience is fragmented (some performing Hajj, others observing); commercial content sees a 5-7 day soft pause
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: muted on luxury/discretionary; elevated on travel/tour packages for the season
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; spiritual content far outperforms commercial
audience_behavior.food_delivery_spike: elevated in Mecca region, baseline elsewhere

year_specific_notes.2026: 8-13 Dhul Hijjah 1447 AH ≈ 25-30 May 2026 per UMQ. Eid al-Adha falls during this window (10 Dhul Hijjah = May 27). Saudi MoH/MoHU typically issues pre-Hajj guidance in early Shawwal.

avoid: mixing UAE flag with non-UAE national symbols in same composition
generic patriotic stock imagery
any framing that singles out one emirate at the expense of the federation theme — Dec 2 is about the union
humor about the ruling families
alcohol references
prefer: UAE flag (red, green, white, black) treated reverently
Sheikh Zayed founding-father imagery (50+ year anniversary themes)
Burj Khalifa light show imagery (where rights-cleared)
traditional dress (kandura, abaya) alongside modern UAE

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evening-heavy; Burj Khalifa light show and Saadiyat/Yas fireworks drive sharp peaks; UAE-wide trends
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for fashion (UAE-color-themed), automotive (major National Day promotions), home decor
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: year's-highest day in UAE; visiting tourist content elevated too

year_specific_notes.2026: 55th National Day (UAE founded Dec 2 1971). Public holiday observed Dec 2-3 standard. Major brand activations typically begin Nov 15 onward.

avoid: promotional/sales-led messaging — this is a day of national mourning, not commercial celebration
imagery that conflates Commemoration Day with National Day (which immediately follows) — they have opposite tonal registers
loud celebratory aesthetics
humor of any kind
prefer: 1-minute-silence imagery (Wahat Al Karama memorial in Abu Dhabi)
respectful UAE flag at half-mast (typical observance protocol)
messages honoring UAE armed forces and emergency services

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: morning-spike unusual for UAE; driven by the official 11:30 1-minute silence observance
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: muted on promotional content; brands typically pause campaigns
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: elevated; tribute content sees high engagement

year_specific_notes.2026: Public holiday observed Dec 1 (since 2019). Flag-at-half-mast 8am-11:30am with national moment of silence at 11:30 happens on the actual commemoration date (Nov 30); the Dec 1 holiday is the day-off observance. See `_entry_notes.uae_commemoration_day.commemorates_event_date` for context.

avoid: partisan or political framing — Flag Day is consensus-national
humor
any imagery showing the flag below ground level or in inappropriate positions
prefer: themes of unity across the 7 emirates
Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid quotes (he founded the day in 2013)
behind-the-scenes flag-design history (designed in 1971 by Abdullah Mohammed Al Maainah at age 19)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: morning peak (unusual for UAE) driven by ceremony coverage; secondary evening peak
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: neutral (no major commercial moment)
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; school/community content dominates

avoid: generic 'sale' messaging that doesn't lean into the DSF brand — DSF is a 30-year-old anchor that audiences expect specific brand cues
any imagery suggesting it's a quiet/curated event — it's a high-energy citywide spectacle
competing with Black Friday framing (DSF positions as the Gulf's equivalent of London/NYC holiday shopping)
prefer: citywide drone light shows and fireworks
raffle/jackpot imagery (cars, gold, cash, vouchers are signature prizes)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evening-heavy; Thursday-Saturday peak; tourist content adds international engagement
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: sharp elevation across all retail categories; international tourist arrivals spike
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: sustained 5-week elevation; concert + raffle reveals drive viral moments

year_specific_notes.2026: 31st edition ran Dec 5 2025 → Jan 11 2026 (~38 days). Future editions follow the same December-start pattern.

avoid: beach/outdoor-activity framing during the hottest hours — temperatures hit 45°C+, audience is indoors
any messaging that treats summer-in-Dubai as a downturn (DSS is engineered to counter the slump)
tourist-only framing — DSS is targeted at residents and intra-GCC travelers, not the December European tourist segment
prefer: air-conditioned indoor experiences (malls, indoor theme parks, hotel staycations)
family-with-kids imagery (school summer break is the target window)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: two-peak daily pattern (mid-morning + late afternoon-evening); audience is families with school-aged children
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated on fashion, kids' goods, electronics; significant staycation booking activity
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; lower-volume than DSF but high-conversion family segment

year_specific_notes.2026: DSS 2026 runs Jul 2 → Aug 30 (~59 days). Organized by Dubai Festivals and Retail Establishment (DFRE).

avoid: modern/futurist aesthetics — this is heritage

any framing that flattens 'Sharjah' into 'Dubai-adjacent' — Sharjah has a distinct cultural identity (UNESCO designated it the cultural capital of the Arab world in 1998 and the Arab capital of culture 2014)
tourist-marketing aesthetic (the festival is for residents + heritage enthusiasts first)
prefer: traditional Emirati crafts (sadu weaving, talli embroidery, palm-frond work)
luqaimat (Emirati sweet dumplings), Arabic coffee ceremonies
international participant pavilions (the festival also showcases other cultures)

audience_behavior.peak_hours: 17:00-22:00 (visitor evenings; pleasant Feb weather enables long outdoor evenings)
audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evening-heavy; weekend spike; family + heritage-interested 30+ segment
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for traditional crafts, regional food products
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; outsized engagement from Sharjah-resident + UAE heritage segments

year_specific_notes.2026: 23rd edition ran Feb 4-15 2026, theme 'Glow of Authenticity'. Held across 7 locations in the emirate (Sharjah City, Khorfakkan, Kalba, Dibba Al Hisn, Al Dhaid, Wadi Al Helo, Al Hamriyah).

avoid: mass-market tone — this is a high-end art fair targeting collectors, galleries, and the international art press
treating the event as 'just another art exhibition' — it is the GCC's flagship contemporary art moment
any imagery without rights clearance for the represented artists/galleries
prefer: Saadiyat Cultural District context (Louvre Abu Dhabi, Zayed Museum)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: high-engagement-per-post but lower volume than mass-market events; international art-collector audience visible in timezone distribution
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: high-value art purchases (the audience is collectors)
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; strong Instagram engagement, lower TikTok

year_specific_notes.2026: First edition under the Frieze brand (announced as 'Frieze Abu Dhabi') runs Nov 19-22 2026 at Manarat Al Saadiyat, replacing the legacy Abu Dhabi Art fair that ran through 2025. Organized by Frieze in partnership with the Department of Culture and Tourism — Abu Dhabi.

avoid: foreign symbols
Saudi-/UAE-styled flag treatments — Qatar's flag (maroon and white with serrated edge) is distinct and culturally specific
conflating with the broader Gulf — Qatar has its own founding narrative
humor about the ruling Al Thani family
alcohol references
prefer: Sheikh Jassim 'The Founder' imagery (he succeeded his father on Dec 18 1878 — that succession is what's commemorated)
modern Doha skyline + traditional dhows juxtaposition
Aspire Tower, Museum of Islamic Art, National Museum imagery

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evening-heavy; Corniche parade and fireworks drive sharp peaks; #اليوم_الوطني_القطري trends
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for fashion (maroon-themed), automotive (National Day promotions are a category), home decor
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: year's-highest day in Qatar; family + brand content dominate

year_specific_notes.2026: 148th anniversary of Sheikh Jassim's 1878 succession. Single-day public holiday (most major brand activations span Dec 15-20 window).

avoid: sedentary lifestyle imagery
indoor-only framing — the day is designed for outdoor + public-space activation
imagery that suggests this is for elite athletes only — it's a participation-for-all day
alcohol references
prefer: Aspire Zone, Lusail Boulevard, public parks activity
traditional Qatari sports (falconry, archery, camel/horse racing)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: morning peak unusual for Qatar — driven by participation event coverage
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for athleisure, sports gear, supplements
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; high participation rate so user-generated content is significant

year_specific_notes.2026: Held Tuesday Feb 10 2026 (2nd Tuesday of February per recurring rule). Amiri Diwan confirmed it as official paid public holiday. 250+ events across public parks/venues.

avoid: modern yacht / water-sports tourism aesthetics
framing the dhow as just decoration — the boats are working maritime craft
tourist-only framing
prefer: inter-Gulf dhow visits from neighboring states

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evening-heavy; weekend (Thu-Sat) spike; family heritage audience
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for traditional crafts, pearl jewelry, maritime-themed home decor
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; high engagement on golden-hour dhow photography

year_specific_notes.2026: Typically late November / early December; usually coincides with Qatar National Day window. Confirm exact 2026 dates via katara.net closer to event.

avoid: high-end retail framing — the Spring Festival is family-carnival positioning, distinct from the Souq's everyday luxury appeal
Eid-style imagery (this festival has its own positioning)
loud nightclub aesthetics
prefer: Souq Waqif heritage architecture as backdrop

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evening-heavy; weekend (Thu-Sat) spike; family + visitor audience
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated near Souq Waqif retail and dining
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; high UGC from family attendees

year_specific_notes.2026: Date varies year-to-year — sometimes runs December/January, sometimes Mar/Apr, often coinciding with school holiday windows. Confirm via visitqatar.com closer to event.

avoid: framing as competing with other GCC book fairs (Sharjah, Riyadh) — Doha's positioning is regional collaboration
western-publishing dominant imagery — the fair is Arabic-language-first
academic-only framing — children's literature is a major draw
prefer: Doha Exhibition and Convention Center (DECC) venue

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: moderate; evening peak for author events; strong Arabic-reader segment
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for book purchases, stationery, children's books
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; high engagement on author quotes / book reveals

year_specific_notes.2026: 35th edition runs May 14-23 2026 at DECC. Registration opened Dec 2025.

avoid: Hollywood red-carpet framing — Ajyal is youth-jury-led and family-oriented, not glamour-driven
framing as competing with regional festivals (Red Sea Film Festival, etc.) — Ajyal's positioning is youth empowerment
violent or mature film stills without context
prefer: youth jury imagery (8-21 year-olds judge selected films)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evening-heavy; weekend spike; youth + cinephile + family segments
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: ticket sales for screenings + DFI memberships
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: elevated on TikTok/Instagram among youth segment
audience_behavior.food_delivery_spike: elevated near Katara during screening hours

avoid: conflating with Liberation Day (Feb 26) — they commemorate distinct historical events
foreign symbols
humor about ruling Al Sabah family
alcohol references (Kuwait is fully dry)
prefer: Kuwait flag (black, white, green, red — distinct from Saudi/UAE)
water-spray celebrations (a famous Kuwaiti tradition where cars/people spray each other with water — uniquely Kuwaiti)

audience_behavior.peak_hours: 10:00-23:00 (high-energy all-day; water-spray streets are a daytime phenomenon)
audience_behavior.engagement_shift: all-day elevation — Kuwaiti National Day is famously high-energy, with kids/youth taking to the streets with water-spray guns
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for fashion (national-color-themed), home decor, automotive promotions
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: year's-highest day in Kuwait; street UGC dominates

year_specific_notes.2026: Kuwait National Day on Wed Feb 25 + Liberation Day on Thu Feb 26 = 4-day weekend (with adjacent weekend). Hala February festival builds month-long energy leading up.

avoid: framing identical to National Day (Feb 25) — Liberation Day has a more reverent, historical tone since it commemorates 1991 liberation from Iraqi occupation
any framing that diminishes the Gulf War history or coalition involvement
humor
imagery suggesting full conflict-imagery — focus on liberation/freedom themes, not war scenes
prefer: Kuwait Towers (struck during invasion, restored after)
tributes to Sheikh Jaber Al-Ahmad (Emir during the invasion era)
Kuwait military and Coalition forces gratitude themes

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: elevated all day; more reflective tone than Feb 25; historical/tribute content outperforms party content
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated (continues from National Day Feb 25)
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: year's high; tribute and historical content outperform

year_specific_notes.2026: 35th anniversary of liberation from Iraqi occupation (Feb 26 1991).

avoid: framing as just a sale — Hala February is a cultural+commercial month built around National Day + Liberation Day
competing with DSF positioning (different country, different cultural moment)
alcohol references
prefer: fashion shows, family entertainment, poetry evenings
Avenues Mall and Salmiya area activations

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: sustained month-long elevation; peaks around Feb 25-26 National/Liberation Days
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: month-long sales drive; peak around the National Day window
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: elevated throughout February in Kuwait

year_specific_notes.2026: Runs early February through end of month. Programming culminates in National Day (25th) + Liberation Day (26th).

avoid: Saudi/UAE styling — Oman has distinct national identity (Ibadi Muslim majority, distinct flag and dress)
comparing Sultan Haitham unfavorably to his predecessor Sultan Qaboos (the transition has been smooth and unified)
humor about ruling family
alcohol references
prefer: Sultan Qaboos legacy imagery (Nov 18 is also his birthday; Renaissance Day legacy)
Oman flag (red, white, green with khanjar dagger emblem)
Royal Opera House Muscat, Sultan Qaboos Grand Mosque
traditional Omani dress (dishdasha, kummah cap, khanjar)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evening-heavy; Muscat Bay fireworks drive peaks; military parade content elevates throughout the day
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for fashion (national-color-themed), automotive, home decor
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: year's-highest day in Oman; tribute content to Sultan Qaboos remains strong even years post-passing

year_specific_notes.2026: Also commemorates Sultan Qaboos's birthday (Nov 18 1940). 2-day public holiday standard (Nov 18-19). 56th National Day.

entries.name_ar: يوم تولي السلطان هيثم بن طارق مقاليد الحكم

avoid: direct comparison to Sultan Qaboos (the predecessor) — the official tone is continuity, not contrast
framing as a 'new beginning' that implies discontinuity
humor about ruling family
alcohol references
prefer: Sultan Haitham bin Tarik portraiture (official photographs)
Vision 2040 themes — this is the modernization-narrative day

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: respectful elevation throughout the day; tribute and Vision-2040 content outperforms
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: neutral (less commercial than National Day)
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; official ceremony content dominates

year_specific_notes.2026: Established as a national holiday by Royal Decree 88/2022 commemorating Sultan Haitham's accession on Jan 11 2020. Observed Thursday Jan 15 2026 per Oman's advance-announcement public-holiday policy (the canonical date stays Jan 11 — government may shift the day-off observance year to year).

avoid: luxury-only positioning — Muscat Festival is a mass family event
Dubai-style mall-centric framing — Muscat Festival is park + heritage venue based
alcohol references
prefer: Qurum Natural Park, Naseem Park venue imagery

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evening-heavy; weekend (Fri-Sat) spike; family audience
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated near festival venues, traditional crafts dominate
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; family UGC dominates

year_specific_notes.2026: Typically runs Jan-Feb spanning Oman's most pleasant weather window. Exact dates confirmed via Ministry of Heritage & Tourism closer to event.

avoid: dry/hot desert imagery — Salalah's whole appeal in this window is the green Khareef transformation (separate shared entry covers the climate; this entry covers the festival)
Muscat-style modern aesthetics (Dhofar has distinct cultural identity)
alcohol references
prefer: frankincense (luban) heritage themes — UNESCO Land of Frankincense site

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evening-heavy; Gulf tourists from UAE/Saudi visible in engagement timing
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for frankincense, perfumery, traditional crafts
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate-to-elevated; cross-GCC tourist content boosts reach
audience_behavior.food_delivery_spike: elevated in Salalah during festival evenings

year_specific_notes.2026: Tied to Khareef season; expected mid-July → end of August 2026. Specific opening date typically announced 2-3 weeks prior by Oman Ministry of Heritage & Tourism.

avoid: lowland Oman framing — this is mountain-specific, Jabal Akhdar (the Green Mountain) is a high-altitude plateau
framing as a generic rose festival — the Damascene rose variety + traditional distillation method are the cultural fingerprint
alcohol references
prefer: high-altitude mountain landscape (Jabal Akhdar is 2,000m elevation)
terraced rose gardens in villages like Al Sayq

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: sunrise-photography content drives unusually strong morning peak; small high-engagement audience
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for rose water, perfumery, mountain-resort bookings
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: low-volume but high-engagement-per-post (niche heritage segment)

year_specific_notes.2026: 2026 edition launched March 30. Rose harvest typically runs mid-March → early May, festival programming spans this window. Expected 400 hiking participants on the Rose Trail.

avoid: conflating with Accession Day (Dec 17, separate adjacent holiday)
Saudi-styled flag treatments (Bahrain flag has serrated edge, distinct)
humor about ruling Al Khalifa family
alcohol references — though Bahrain permits alcohol licensed venues, National Day messaging should be inclusive

year_specific_notes.2026: 55th National Day (independence 1971). 2-day public holiday window (Dec 16 + Dec 17 Accession Day).

avoid: partisan or political framing
comparing to predecessor
humor about monarchy
alcohol references on the day itself

year_specific_notes.2026: King Hamad ascended Dec 17 1999. 27 years of his reign in 2026.

avoid: mass-commercial framing — Spring of Culture is the high-art Bahrain Authority for Culture flagship
Saudi/UAE-styled large-spectacle aesthetics — this is a curated, salon-style festival
alcohol references at family programming
prefer: classical concerts (Bahrain Bay, Cultural Hall venues)
international collaborations (the festival partners with embassies of China, France, Germany, India, Italy, Palestine)

year_specific_notes.2026: 20th edition was planned for Mar-May 2026, but has been postponed by the Spring of Culture organizers (no rescheduled dates announced as of curation time). See _entry_notes.

avoid: modern aesthetics — heritage is the entire point
framing Bahrain as 'Dubai-adjacent'
alcohol references
prefer: traditional Bahraini crafts (naseej weaving, maliyah pottery, palm-frond work)

avoid: outdoor-activity framing — summer in Bahrain hits 45°C+, audience is indoors
comparing to DSS (different country, different positioning)
alcohol references at family programming

audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for kids' goods, family-day-out tickets

entries.name_en: Bahrain Food Festival (Flavors Around the World)

avoid: dieting/restriction framing — the festival is celebration-oriented
framing Bahraini cuisine as identical to other Gulf cuisines (Bahrain has distinct dishes like machboos, halwa Bahraini, samboosa)
alcohol references at family programming
prefer: Marassi Al Bahrain (Diyar Muharraq) venue imagery
Bahraini specialties (machboos, ghoozi, halwa Bahraini, samboosa) alongside global cuisines

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: lunch + dinner peaks; weekend (Fri-Sat) spike; foodie + family segments
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for dining bookings, specialty food retail
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: elevated on Instagram (food photography)
audience_behavior.food_delivery_spike: lower than baseline (people are dining at the festival)

year_specific_notes.2026: Held February 2026 (~20 days) at Marassi Al Bahrain (Diyar Muharraq). Branded as 'Flavors Around the World' in some years.

avoid: outdoor lifestyle photography during midday hours
imagery that implies comfort being outdoors mid-day
running/cycling imagery without 'early morning / post-sunset' context
promotional content for outdoor restaurants without indoor option
any framing that treats 50°C as 'just hot'
prefer: indoor venues (malls, hotels, indoor theme parks)
summer travel destinations (Salalah, Europe, Caucasus, Türkiye)

audience_behavior.peak_hours: 10:00-12:00 + 17:00-23:00 (audience is indoors; engagement shifts to climate-controlled hours)
audience_behavior.engagement_shift: two-peak daily pattern; outdoor influencer content collapses; staycation + travel content surges
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for AC appliances, summer travel, indoor entertainment, cooling skincare; muted for outdoor goods
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: high; travel-out-of-GCC and indoor-staycation content dominate
audience_behavior.food_delivery_spike: extreme all day (heat strongly suppresses dine-out behavior)

year_specific_notes.2026: Peak heat window typically late June through late August. Hajj falls in this window for 2026 (May 25-30), so Eid al-Adha messaging overlaps the early ramp-up.

avoid: nostalgic framing aimed at older audiences without kids — back-to-school is for parents with school-age children
imagery that excludes parents of younger kindergarten ages (a major segment)
Western academic-year framing — GCC schools largely follow Aug/Sep start

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: morning-peak driven by parent prep; weekend-heavy for shopping trips
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: sharp elevation for stationery, kids fashion, electronics; lasts ~6 weeks
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: elevated; first-day photos dominate parent feeds
audience_behavior.food_delivery_spike: moderate (lunchbox prep means home cooking, but evening orders up)

year_specific_notes.2026: Most GCC schools restart late August / early September. Public-school start dates set by each country's Ministry of Education; private schools vary by curriculum (British: early Sep, American: late Aug, IB: varies).

avoid: winter-coat / snow-boot framing (GCC winter is 15-25°C, not cold by Western standards)
framing as 'cold season' — most GCC residents see this as the comfortable outdoor window
indoor-only positioning (the season's whole point is being outdoors)
prefer: desert camping (overnight tents, fire pits, stargazing)
hiking and adventure tourism (UAE wadis, Saudi mountains, Omani trails)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: two-peak daily; outdoor photography content surges; opposite pattern from summer
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for outdoor gear, camping equipment, light-layering fashion, weekend hotel stays
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: highest engagement window of the year for outdoor lifestyle content

year_specific_notes.2026: Dec 2026 → Feb 2027 window. Coincides with Riyadh Season, Diriyah Season, DSF, Winter at Tantora, and Sharjah Heritage Days — multiple country-pinned events leverage this climate window.

avoid: the 'Black' framing in Arabic-targeted content — 'الجمعة السوداء' carries negative connotations (death, mourning); the regional adaptation is 'الجمعة البيضاء' (White Friday), pioneered by Souq.com in 2014 and now adopted across GCC retail
framing as exclusively a Western import (regional retailers have made it their own)
gory or black-coded aesthetics
imagery suggesting scarcity-driven panic shopping
prefer: WHITE Friday (الجمعة البيضاء) branding in Arabic

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: extreme spike on Friday and the surrounding week; midnight drops drive viral moments
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: year's-highest commercial day for many retailers; cross-border online purchases spike
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: extreme; influencer haul content dominates

year_specific_notes.2026: Black/White Friday falls Nov 27 2026. Cyber Monday Nov 30. Many GCC retailers extend to a full White Friday Week or Month. Note: Nov 30 is also UAE Commemoration Day — UAE-targeted campaigns should pause promotional content on that day.

avoid: framing as competing with Islamic New Year (rأs al-sana al-hijriyya) — they're distinct events with distinct audiences
alcohol-heavy imagery for KSA/Kuwait/Qatar-targeted content (all three are dry markets) — UAE/Bahrain have more latitude
religious framing of any kind (NYE is secular)
prefer: fireworks displays (Burj Khalifa, Atlantis the Royal, Qatar Corniche are headline)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evening + post-midnight spike; UAE biggest in GCC for NYE entertainment given regulatory latitude
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for hospitality bookings, evening wear, gift purchases
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: year's-highest single moment for short-video content (fireworks/countdown clips)

year_specific_notes.2026: NYE Dec 31 2026 is a Thursday → Jan 1 2027 (Friday, weekend) creates an extended celebration window.

avoid: framing as Oman-domestic-only — the Khareef tourism economy is heavily fueled by GCC travelers, particularly UAE residents escaping summer heat
dry/desert imagery (the whole appeal is the green monsoon transformation)
framing as exotic 'jungle' — the imagery should be Salalah-authentic
alcohol references
prefer: lush greenery, waterfalls (Wadi Darbat, Ayn Athum, Ayn Razat)

audience_behavior.peak_hours: Sunrise + sunset photography dominates; weekend (Thu-Sun) booking peaks
audience_behavior.engagement_shift: outsized cross-GCC reach for an Oman event — UAE-based engagement is a major contributor
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: high-value cross-border travel bookings; Oman Air launched direct DXB-Salalah for the season
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: high engagement; travel-influencer content spikes mid-July to mid-August
audience_behavior.food_delivery_spike: elevated in Salalah during peak tourism weeks

year_specific_notes.2026: Khareef officially Jun 21 → Sep 20 2026. Peak greenery mid-Jul → mid-Aug. Oman Air launched 3x weekly DXB-Salalah direct from Jul 3 2026.

avoid: budget framing — F1 audience skews luxury
imagery that excludes the regional element (yas marina, etihad towers backdrop matters)
framing as competing with Saudi/Bahrain GPs — the three are season anchors not rivals
alcohol-only celebration content (UAE has latitude but inclusive content performs better)
prefer: evening night-race photography (Abu Dhabi is iconic for sunset → night transition)
season finale framing (typically last race of F1 calendar)

audience_behavior.peak_hours: 16:00-00:00 (race weekend) — Friday practice, Saturday quali, Sunday race + concert
audience_behavior.engagement_shift: weekend-concentrated; international audience visible in timezone profile
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: high-value hospitality bookings (Yas Marina paddock, Yas Hotel, yacht spots)
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: outsized international engagement; driver-meet content drives viral moments
audience_behavior.food_delivery_spike: elevated near Yas Island during race weekend

year_specific_notes.2026: Typically the final race of the F1 calendar (early-to-mid December). Yasalam after-race concert series (Coldplay, Kendrick Lamar, etc. in past editions) extends the weekend. Confirm exact dates via the official F1 calendar.

avoid: alcohol-themed celebration content (Saudi is dry — celebration imagery should be non-alcohol)
outdated 'Saudi as conservative' framing — the Saudi GP is a Vision-2030 entertainment-opening showcase
framing as derivative of Abu Dhabi or Bahrain GPs
imagery that hides Saudi cultural elements behind generic luxury aesthetics
prefer: Jeddah Corniche Circuit (fastest street circuit in F1)
night-race photography under Jeddah Tower/Corniche lighting
post-race concert imagery (Saudi GP weekend includes high-profile concerts)
Saudi flag treatments alongside racing aesthetics

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: weekend-concentrated; large Saudi domestic audience + international race traveller mix
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: high-value hospitality bookings (Jeddah Corniche paddock, Ritz-Carlton, premium hotels)
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: elevated; driver content drives viral moments in Saudi market
audience_behavior.food_delivery_spike: elevated in Jeddah during race weekend

year_specific_notes.2026: Jeddah Corniche Circuit. Typically held March or April. Confirm exact 2026 dates via the official F1 calendar.
